# Field Service Reference

## Overview

This reference collects the diagnostic trouble codes that field technicians consult when servicing the fleet. Each entry below is intentionally near-identical in wording so that the exact alphanumeric fault code is the only thing that distinguishes one entry from another. A single wrong character sends the technician to the wrong recovery procedure, so always confirm the code before acting on it in the field.

## Coolant sensor fault

Fault ZX9-4471Q indicates that a monitored sensor has drifted out of its calibrated tolerance band and the affected unit must be recalibrated before it will leave limp-home mode. Clear the latched diagnostic flag only after the full recalibration handshake completes successfully, otherwise the same fault re-arms on the next cold start and the underlying drift stays hidden from the technician on the following service visit.
